items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket permits public write access. It evaluates the block public access settings, the bucket policy, and the bucket access control list (ACL). The control fails if the bucket permits public write access.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public write access

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket permits public write access. It evaluates the block public access settings, the bucket policy, and the bucket access control list (ACL). The control fails if the bucket permits public write access.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public write access

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket permits public read access. It evaluates the block public access settings, the bucket policy, and the bucket access control list (ACL). The control fails if the bucket permits public read access.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public read access

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket permits public read access. It evaluates the block public access settings, the bucket policy, and the bucket access control list (ACL). The control fails if the bucket permits public read access.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public read access

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket has a Lifecycle configuration. The control fails if the bucket doesn't have a Lifecycle configuration. You can provide custom values for one or more if the preceding parameters, the control passes only if the policy includes the specified storage class, deletion time, and transition time.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should have Lifecycle configurations

items.description: This control checks whether server access logging is enabled for an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket. The control fails if server access logging isn't enabled. When logging is enabled, Amazon S3 delivers access logs for a source bucket to a chosen target bucket. The target bucket must be in the same AWS Region as the source bucket and must not have a default retention period configured. The target logging bucket does not need to have server access logging enabled, and you should suppress findings for this bucket.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should have server access logging enabled

items.description: This control checks whether unrestricted incoming traffic for an Amazon EC2 security group is accessible to the specified ports [3389, 20, 23, 110, 143, 3306, 8080, 1433, 9200, 9300, 25, 445, 135, 21, 1434, 4333, 5432, 5500, 5601, 22, 3000, 5000, 8088, 8888] that are considered to be high risk. This control fails if any of the rules in a security group allow ingress traffic from '0.0.0.0/0' or '::/0' to those ports.
items.status_message: The fix was applied successfully. Restricted access was added, but unrestricted public access is still present. Remove the unrestricted rule to resolve this finding.
items.title: Security groups should not allow unrestricted access to ports with high risk

items.description: This control checks whether unrestricted incoming traffic for an Amazon EC2 security group is accessible to the specified ports [3389, 20, 23, 110, 143, 3306, 8080, 1433, 9200, 9300, 25, 445, 135, 21, 1434, 4333, 5432, 5500, 5601, 22, 3000, 5000, 8088, 8888] that are considered to be high risk. This control fails if any of the rules in a security group allow ingress traffic from '0.0.0.0/0' or '::/0' to those ports.
items.status_message: The fix was applied successfully. Restricted access was added, but unrestricted public access is still present. Remove the unrestricted rule to resolve this finding.
items.title: Security groups should not allow unrestricted access to ports with high risk

items.description: This control checks whether AWS Config is enabled in your account in the current AWS Region, records all resources that correspond to controls that are enabled in the current Region, and uses the service-linked AWS Config role.
items.pending_confirmation_message: The fix was applied successfully, but AWS source-of-truth confirmation has not arrived after 12 hours. Check Security Hub/reconciliation and investigate why the resolved state has not propagated.
items.status_message: The fix was applied successfully, but AWS source-of-truth confirmation has not arrived after 12 hours. Check Security Hub/reconciliation and investigate why the resolved state has not propagated.
items.title: AWS Config should be enabled and use the service-linked role for resource recording

items.description: This AWS control checks whether the root user access key is available.
items.title: IAM root user access key should not exist

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group permits unrestricted incoming traffic from unauthorized ports. The control status is determined as follows: If you use the default value for 'authorizedTcpPorts', the control fails if the security group permits unrestricted incoming traffic from any port other than ports 80 and 443; If you provide custom values for 'authorizedTcpPorts' or 'authorizedUdpPorts', the control fails if the security group permits unrestricted incoming traffic from any unlisted port; If no parameter is used, the control fails for any security group that has an unrestricted inbound traffic rule.
items.status_message: The fix was applied successfully. Restricted access was added, but unrestricted public access is still present. Remove the unrestricted rule to resolve this finding.
items.title: Security groups should only allow unrestricted incoming traffic for authorized ports

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group permits unrestricted incoming traffic from unauthorized ports. The control status is determined as follows: If you use the default value for 'authorizedTcpPorts', the control fails if the security group permits unrestricted incoming traffic from any port other than ports 80 and 443; If you provide custom values for 'authorizedTcpPorts' or 'authorizedUdpPorts', the control fails if the security group permits unrestricted incoming traffic from any unlisted port; If no parameter is used, the control fails for any security group that has an unrestricted inbound traffic rule.
items.status_message: The fix was applied successfully. Restricted access was added, but unrestricted public access is still present. Remove the unrestricted rule to resolve this finding.
items.title: Security groups should only allow unrestricted incoming traffic for authorized ports

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. The control fails if the security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22.
items.status_message: The fix was applied successfully. Restricted access was added, but unrestricted public access is still present. Remove the unrestricted rule to resolve this finding.
items.title: Security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 to port 22

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. The control fails if the security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22.
items.status_message: The fix was applied successfully. Restricted access was added, but unrestricted public access is still present. Remove the unrestricted rule to resolve this finding.
items.title: Security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 to port 22

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket blocks public access at the bucket level. The control fails if any of the following settings are set to false: ignorePublicAcls, blockPublicPolicy, blockPublicAcls, restrictPublicBuckets.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public access

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket blocks public access at the bucket level. The control fails if any of the following settings are set to false: ignorePublicAcls, blockPublicPolicy, blockPublicAcls, restrictPublicBuckets.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public access

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket has a policy that requires requests to use SSL. The control fails if the bucket policy doesn't require requests to use SSL.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should require requests to use SSL

items.description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket is encrypted with an AWS KMS key (SSE-KMS or DSSE-KMS). The control fails if the bucket is encrypted with default encryption (SSE-S3).
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should be encrypted at rest with AWS KMS keys

items.description: This control checks whether server access logging is enabled for an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket. The control fails if server access logging isn't enabled. When logging is enabled, Amazon S3 delivers access logs for a source bucket to a chosen target bucket. The target bucket must be in the same AWS Region as the source bucket and must not have a default retention period configured. The target logging bucket does not need to have server access logging enabled, and you should suppress findings for this bucket.
items.title: S3 general purpose buckets should have server access logging enabled
